structured.title: Rachel Reviews Worship And Resurrection
structured.overview: Rachel and the user work through a theology outline, likely for a church or Bible study lesson. The discussion centers on 1 Corinthians 14 and 15: orderly worship should prevent competition, promote edification, and build a loving church culture where love is the true measure of maturity. They also review how 1 Corinthians 15 defends the bodily resurrection of Christ and the future resurrection of believers, noting that some Corinthians denied the future bodily resurrection because of worldly philosophical assumptions.

apps_results.content:
## Orderly worship
- Structure worship services
- Balance spiritual gifts
- Prevent competition

## Church maturity
- Promote edification
- Build loving culture
- Love measures maturity

## 1 Corinthians 15
- Defends Christ’s bodily resurrection
- Defends believers’ future resurrection
- Some Corinthians denied resurrection

## Reason for denial
- Worldly philosophical assumptions
- Rejected future bodily resurrection
- Needed correction

## Next steps
- Review 1 Corinthians 14–15
- Focus on orderly worship
- Emphasize resurrection truth
